home.name: Robert Bitlev
home.role: SPbU student focused on mathematical modeling and computational methods
home.activityLine: I explore computational approaches to scientific problems.
home.studentLine: B.Sc. student in “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” at St. Petersburg State University.

paragraphs: My name is Robert Bitlev. I study at the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science in the “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” program at St. Petersburg State University. I am most interested in problems at the intersection of mathematics, computational methods, and experimental science.

Alongside olympiad training and university study, I have worked on research tasks related to materials, radio electronics, Arduino prototyping, and scientific analysis. In this work, I used computational tools to structure complex ideas, analyze results, and make conclusions clearer and better grounded.
I am especially drawn to environments where scientific rigor, hands-on experimentation, and clear technical communication come together.

home.educationIntro: The focus here is on scientific training, experimental work, and computational breadth.
home.achievementsIntro: Here the focus shifts toward physics, the exact sciences, and interdisciplinary competition results.
home.projectsIntro: These projects were selected to highlight scientific curiosity, engineering experimentation, and interdisciplinary modeling.
home.skillsIntro: Here computational skills are presented as tools for scientific and technical work.

sidebar.title: Achievements
sidebar.heading: Olympiads and science competitions
bullets: Gold medalist at the International Junior Science Olympiad (IJSO) in 2023 and 2024.
Winner and prize-winner of the final stage of the All-Russian Olympiad for School Students in Physics.
Winner of the Moscow Olympiad in Physics in 2023 and 2024.
Final-stage participant in the IChem Prize in 2024.

sidebar.title: Interests
sidebar.heading: Natural sciences and technology
sidebar.text: Interested in problems at the intersection of physics, mathematics, engineering, and software tools for research.

document.role: AI360 Student | Physics and Applied Electronics
document.summary: Student in the “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” program at the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science at St. Petersburg State University, with a strong background in physics, mathematics, and applied electronics. My olympiad, research, and interdisciplinary projects combine experimental work, device development, data analysis, and software tools.

projects.title: Radio: Bridge over Waves
projects.subtitle: Speech recording and recognition system
bullets: Built two Arduino modules: a transmitter with a microphone, encryption, and a 2.4 GHz radio link, and a receiver that sends data to a server over USB.
Built a three-stage Python pipeline for restoring missing samples, saving WAV files, and converting audio to text through a cloud API and an offline recognizer.

projects.title: Thin Films from Silver Nanoparticles
projects.subtitle: SERS films for sensors
bullets: Synthesized silver nanoparticles by hydroxylamine reduction and formed SERS films with transfer onto silanized substrates.
Characterized the samples with UV-Vis and AFM, estimated an average particle size of about 79 nm, and assessed coating uniformity and film defects.

projects.title: Brain Train
projects.subtitle: Interactive memory trainer on Arduino Nano
bullets: Designed an Arduino Nano device with random LED sequences, OLED feedback, a hint system, and button input for training attention and memory.
Combined four Arduino C++ modules — game logic, button handling, result display, and timing control — to make the training loop more stable and responsive.

projects.title: Modern Systematics of Protists
projects.subtitle: Research and cataloging project
bullets: Prepared a catalog of unicellular organisms that takes taxonomic revisions and recent changes in protist systematics into account.
Structured the biological data into a convenient classification base for educational and research use.

courses: Presidential Physics and Mathematics Lyceum No. 239, 2019–2025
School of Practical Programming and Data Analysis
Experimental physics and non-standard problem solving

career.title: Career Path
career.name: Robert Bitlev
career.role: SPbU student focused on mathematical modeling and computational methods

careerPath.intro: This STEM path is built around olympiad physics, experimental projects, and computational methods that reinforce one another.

pillars.title: Scientific foundation
pillars.text: Physics and the exact sciences gave me a foundation for rigorous thinking, careful work with details, and confident problem solving.

pillars.title: Experience across disciplines
pillars.text: My experience includes projects at the intersection of radio electronics, new materials, biological cataloging, Arduino prototyping, and data analysis.

pillars.title: Computational methods
pillars.text: AI360 and my work with Python, SQL, and machine learning complement my scientific background and help me work confidently with modeling, data analysis, and research problems.

timeline.title: Physics Center at Presidential Lyceum No. 239
timeline.text: Studied physics and mathematics in depth: solved non-standard problems, worked on experimental physics, and learned mathematical methods used in physics.

timeline.title: Modern Systematics of Protists
timeline.text: Prepared a catalog of unicellular organisms that reflects changes in modern biological systematics.

timeline.title: Arduino and radio electronics
timeline.text: Worked on Arduino projects, including Brain Train, and on capturing and processing text from a remote microphone.

timeline.title: International science olympiads
timeline.text: Won IJSO gold medals in 2023 and 2024, as well as a gold medal at the International Olympiad in Exact Sciences in 2022.

timeline.title: Physics olympiads
timeline.text: Was a winner and prize-winner of the final stage of the All-Russian Olympiad in Physics and also won the Moscow Olympiad in Physics.

timeline.title: Thin films from silver nanoparticles
timeline.text: Prepared a materials-focused project for the IChem Prize and Big Challenges competitions.

timeline.title: AI360 at St. Petersburg State University
timeline.text: I continue developing in mathematics, machine learning, modeling, and computational methods.

highlights.title: International results
highlights.text: Gold medals at IJSO in 2023 and 2024, as well as gold at the International Olympiad in Exact Sciences in 2024.

highlights.title: Physics olympiads
highlights.text: Winner and prize-winner results in major physics olympiads over several years.

highlights.title: Materials, electronics, and biology
highlights.text: Projects on silver nanoparticles, radio signal processing, Arduino devices, and modern protist systematics show a broad range of scientific interests.

focus.title: Modeling and machine learning
focus.text: I am interested in problems at the intersection of scientific research, data analysis, mathematical modeling, and machine learning.

focus.title: Radio electronics and physical experiments
focus.text: I keep an interest in applied physics through radio electronics, measurement, and hardware prototypes.

focus.title: Programming as a research tool
focus.text: I use programming and algorithms as working tools for analysis, modeling, and interdisciplinary problem solving.
